title: T-111 — D365 Sync Config screen (SET-082 §11.3)
prompt:
# T-111 — D365 Sync Config screen (SET-082 §11.3)

PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §11.3 (D365 Admin UI)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Build SET-082 D365 Sync Config — manages pull schedule (cron), push queue settings, retry policy, and DLQ view link. Persists via T-030 d365 config Server Actions. RBAC `owner` only per §11.4.

## Implementation contract
1. Page at apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/integrations/d365/sync/page.tsx (Server Component) reads sync config row (singleton per org from `d365_sync_config` table per T-030 schema).
2. Form sections: Pull schedule (cron input + next-run preview), Push queue (max_inflight, batch_size), Retry policy (max_attempts, backoff_ms), DLQ (link to admin DLQ tooling — out of UI scope here).
3. RHF + Zod resolver; Submit calls T-030 updateD365SyncConfig action.
4. Show last applied at + applied_by_user metadata at top.
5. RBAC: requires `owner` role; 403 otherwise.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/integrations/d365/sync/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/integrations/d365/sync/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: RTL test mounts form with mocked config; asserts Zod validation on bad cron + submit calls T-030 action.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/integrations/d365/sync/page.test.tsx`
2. RED must fail.
3. GREEN: implement Server Component + RHF form + submit wiring.
4. Re-run RED + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (§0).
- Do not store credentials in this form (§11.6 V-SET-51 — handled by SET-080 connection screen).
- Do not bypass owner-only RBAC.

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:27-103`
**Label:** `d365_connection_screen`
**Component type:** form

> **No exact prototype** — closest visual pattern: `d365_connection_screen` at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:27-103`. Build in the same structural style as this screen. Re-use the Section + SRow form pattern for the cron/queue/retry config groups; same form footer (Test/Save actions) and same alert banner density for last-applied metadata.

### Parity requirements (mandatory ACs)
The production implementation MUST match the prototype on three dimensions — add these as explicit ACs:
1. **Structural parity** — same sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives invoked, same modals triggered from same actions.
2. **Visual parity** — same density tokens, same shadcn/Radix primitives (no drift to plain HTML), same layout proportions.
3. **Interaction parity** — same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order.

### Translation notes (from prototype-labels index)
- Local useState per credential field → useForm + zodResolver; URL validated with z.string().url().includes('dynamics.com'), tenantId with z.string().uuid()
- window.SETTINGS_D365 mock → Drizzle query on d365_config table (single-row config)
- Password field 'Rotate secret' → separate Server Action calling Azure Key Vault rotation API; never expose secret client-side
- Toggle for integration enabled → Server Action that first calls test-connection (SM-08 pattern) then persists flag
- Client-side URL/cron/UUID validation → mirror with zod .superRefine() for server-side; cron validated with cron-parser
- Hardcoded LEGACY banner → feature flag check integration.d365.legacy_mode from flags table
- Hardcoded labels → next-intl keys

### shadcn primitives to use
`Form`, `Input`, `Select`, `Switch`, `Button`, `Alert`, `Badge`

pipeline_inputs.description: D365 Sync Config (SET-082): cron pull schedule + push queue + retry policy form, owner-only RBAC.
pipeline_inputs.details:
PRD §11.3 SET-082: `Pull schedule (cron), push queue, retry policy, dead-letter queue view`. DLQ deep view itself is out of scope — only a link is rendered (admin DLQ tooling lives in worker package). Cron string validated client-side with cron-parser style Zod refinement; next-run preview rendered using a small in-repo cron evaluator.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given a sync config exists with last_applied_at set, when the page renders, then header strip shows that timestamp and the applied_by_user.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Given the form renders for an owner, when an invalid cron string '*/x * * * *' is entered, then Zod validation surfaces an inline error and Submit is disabled.
Given a valid cron '0 * * * *' is entered with batch_size=100 and max_attempts=3, when Submit is clicked, then T-030 updateD365SyncConfig is called with those values and a success toast renders.
Given the caller is not `owner`, when the page is requested, then a 403 page renders.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given a sync config exists with last_applied_at set, when the page renders, then header strip shows that timestamp and the applied_by_user.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL test asserts cron validation, submit wiring, RBAC 403, header metadata.
GREEN: implement Server Component + RHF + Zod cron refinement + submit handler.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not store credentials in this form (§11.6 V-SET-51 — handled by SET-080 connection screen).
